name: Stockmens National Bank In Cotulla
description_short: Locally owned community bank in Cotulla, TX, established in 1904, offering traditional banking services with face-to-face customer relationships and online banking options.
description_long: Stockmens National Bank in Cotulla was founded in 1904 and has continuously served LaSalle County and surrounding areas for over 120 years. The bank was originally chartered by L.A. Kerr, C.C. Thomas, G.W. Henrichson, George Copp, and T.R. Keck, and has remained rooted in local ownership throughout its history. Currently, approximately 94% of the bank's stock is owned by local residents and their families, including descendants of key stockholders from the 1990s transition period. The bank operates from its third location since inception, a building completed in 1951 on North Main Street that has been remodeled twice and expanded with a four-lane motor bank facility. SNB Cotulla offers comprehensive retail banking services including online banking with mobile app access, bill pay functionality, check ordering, and e-delivery options. The bank emphasizes cybersecurity education for customers through published safety tips and fraud prevention guidance. What distinguishes SNB Cotulla from larger regional or national banks is its commitment to local decision-making and face-to-face customer interactions. The bank explicitly markets itself as "customer driven" and "locally owned," positioning itself as a responsible community business leader. Their website features community resources, cattle and oil price tracking relevant to the local agricultural and energy economy, and integration with local institutions like the Brush Country Chamber of Commerce and Cotulla ISD. As a community bank serving a rural Texas county, SNB Cotulla provides essential banking infrastructure for the region with personalized service. However, the bank does not advertise specialized loan products, investment services, or credit card offerings on their public-facing website, suggesting their focus is traditional core banking services rather than comprehensive financial solutions. Limited online information about rates, fees, or product details is available.
services: Online banking with customer portal login and registration
Mobile banking app (available on Google Play and Apple App Store)
Drive-through motor bank with four traffic lanes
Wire transfer and money movement services
Cybersecurity education and fraud monitoring guidance
pros: Over 120 years of continuous operation and local market presence since 1904
94% locally owned by LaSalle County residents and their families—genuine community ownership
Face-to-face decision-making across the desk with customers, not centralized corporate processing
Mobile app and online banking with bill pay, e-delivery, and mobile deposit options
Proactive cybersecurity education with published 8-tip guides for safe online banking
Four-lane motor bank facility with extended drive-through convenience
Active community engagement tied to local agriculture and energy industries
cons: Limited product transparency—website does not display current rates, fees, or loan terms
No advertised specialty services (credit cards, investment products, wealth management) beyond basic banking
Single location in Cotulla with no branch network for customers outside LaSalle County
Minimal online account opening or self-service options compared to larger institutions
Easter holiday closure at noon on Friday indicates seasonal/limited weekend hours
best_for: Local LaSalle County residents and business owners seeking personalized community banking relationships
Agricultural and ranching operations needing a bank understanding local cattle and oil markets
Customers who prefer face-to-face interactions and local decision-making over corporate banking
Small to mid-sized local businesses requiring traditional checking, savings, and basic lending
diagnosis: SNB Cotulla is best for customers in LaSalle County, Texas, and surrounding areas who value local ownership, personalized service, and community-focused banking over product breadth or digital-first convenience. The primary caveat is that as a small rural community bank, it lacks the service depth, rate competitiveness, and online transparency of larger regional or national banks, making it most suitable for relationship-based banking rather than rate shopping or specialized financial products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Locally owned community bank in Cotulla, TX, established in 1904, offering traditional banking with face-to-face relationships and online banking.
